issues.criterion: The comparison artifact's four finding-transition classes (new/resolved/reopened/stale, ecosystem/07 Sec 3) must each be genuinely, correctly populated -- not merely non-empty -- so a `new` finding has genuine absence-at-baseline-1 proof, a `resolved` finding has real closure evidence, and `stale`/`reopened` are not misapplied.
issues.evidence: Extracted verification entry 16's exact python check (via json.load, never transcribed) and ran it against a deliberately MISCLASSIFIED delta.json fixture built in the evaluator's own scratchpad (never touching the real repo): NDEBT-002 (in reality already RESOLVED at revision e73cd04, confirmed by `git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md` -- appears at line 35, AFTER the `## Resolved` heading at line 29, i.e. it was closed well before either baseline) placed in the `new` array with a comment openly disclosing the fabrication; NDEBT-017 (in reality genuinely new/open, confirmed absent from `git show e73cd04:...DEBT.md` and present in the current tree, per this contract's own entry 11) placed in the `resolved` array with ZERO closure_evidence at the delta level; NDEBT-018 (also genuinely new) placed in `stale`. Entry 16's own check logic -- which only asserts top-level key presence, `len(new)>=1`, `len(resolved)>=1`, `len(stale)>=1`, `reopened==[]`, and that `score_movement.cited_findings` ids exist in ANY findings group -- printed 'ENTRY-16-CHECK-LOGIC: PASS (on a deliberately MISCLASSIFIED / fabricated fixture)'. No other of the 25 verification entries cross-checks which SPECIFIC finding id must land in which SPECIFIC transition bucket, nor that a delta.json `resolved` entry carries its own closure evidence (schema/engineering_finding.schema.json's closure_evidence requirement binds findings.json, a DIFFERENT artifact, with no verification entry tying the two together by id). Also separately confirmed (per the reviewer's F3-gaming probe): verification entry 24 (the only findings.json content check) collects each finding's confidence/status into a dict but asserts nothing about which specific id must carry which specific confidence level -- a generator could dishonestly mark F3 (NDEBT-016, contractually required to stay 'Suspected'/flagged `stale` per design_notes.finding_sourcing_and_confidence_discipline and the stale-evidence non-reuse rule, ecosystem/07 Sec 5) as 'Confirmed' with a freshly-stamped evidence timestamp, without ever re-running the underlying regex verification, and no verification entry would catch it.
issues.required_fix: Add verification entries (extending or replacing entry 16, and amending entry 24) that assert EXACT set membership per transition class -- e.g. `findings['new'] ids == {'NDEBT-017','NDEBT-018'}`, `findings['resolved'] ids == {<the genuinely in-window-resolved set, see the separate resolved/NDEBT-002 issue below>}`, `findings['stale'] ids == {'NDEBT-016'}` -- and that every id in `findings['resolved']` carries non-empty, path-referenced, revision-pinned closure evidence AT THE DELTA LEVEL (or an explicit, checked cross-reference into findings.json's own closure_evidence for the same id). Add an assertion pinning NDEBT-016's confidence to exactly 'Suspected' in findings.json (entry 24), so a silently-upgraded confidence claim on stale-flagged evidence is caught.

issues.criterion: Observation B (Mode A, forwarded for Mode B/evaluator confirmation): delta.json/report.md must not misrepresent NDEBT-002 as an in-window resolution.
issues.evidence: Confirmed via real git history (`git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md`) that NDEBT-002 was ALREADY in the Resolved section at baseline #1's own pinned revision (e73cd04) -- it did not transition from open to resolved during, or because of, this comparison's window; it was resolved on 2026-07-10, well before either baseline (2026-07-17). Per ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md Sec 3, the `resolved` transition class means 'present in the EARLIER execution [i.e. as an open/live finding], absent from the later one' -- but there is no earlier execution's findings.json at all (baseline #1 never ran an audit; it is a preflight+baseline artifact only, no findings array), and even substituting DEBT.md-at-e73cd04 as the earlier reference point (a reasonable proxy, since baseline.json's own planning_references category cites DEBT.md at a pinned revision), NDEBT-002 was ALREADY resolved there too -- so classifying it into delta.json's `resolved` TRANSITION bucket is a category error: nothing actually transitioned in this window. Verification entry 16's own comment text hard-codes this expectation ('expected at least one resolved finding (NDEBT-002)'), which is the ONLY resolved-status finding in the whole 5-finding set (entry 24 confirms only one resolved-status finding exists), so the contract's own acceptance criteria FORCE this exact misrepresentation risk into the real artifact. No verification entry (16, 17, or 18) asserts any disclosure phrase or structural field distinguishing 'previously resolved, pre-baseline-1' from an in-window transition; entry 17 (report.md content check) only requires that report.md name findings.json/delta.json/the first execution-id by path, nothing about this framing distinction; entry 18 (ROADMAP.md shape) requires the SAME four-class-counts-with-ids bullet be added to the tracked, human-facing ROADMAP.md, propagating the same unguarded misrepresentation risk into planning documentation, not just an internal audit artifact.
issues.required_fix: Either (a) do not classify NDEBT-002 in delta.json's finding-transition `resolved` bucket at all -- keep it solely in `unchanged_facts` (as design_notes.unchanged_and_changed_baseline_facts itself already independently supports) and source a genuinely in-window-resolved finding for the `resolved` transition-class demonstration if one exists, or explicitly and honestly disclose (mirroring the `reopened_class_is_mechanism_only` pattern already used for the empty reopened[] array) that no real in-window `resolved` transition exists yet and only a disposable fixture proves the mechanism; or (b) if NDEBT-002 is deliberately retained in the `resolved` bucket to demonstrate the closure-only-with-evidence rule with a real example, add a REQUIRED, exact-text-pinned framing assertion (per the contract-040 closed-shape-pinning precedent, NDEBT-014 item 5) in delta.json (e.g. a required field such as `transition_note: "previously resolved (pre-baseline-1)"`) AND in report.md AND in the ROADMAP.md bullet, each backed by its own new verification entry checking the literal phrase is present adjacent to NDEBT-002's mention.

issues.criterion: docs/planning/ROADMAP.md's addition must be bounded (<=25 added lines, per the contract-040/NDEBT-014-item-5 closed-shape precedent) -- a load-bearing anti-drift cap, not merely additive.
issues.evidence: Ran verification entry 18's exact command (extracted verbatim) in a disposable clone (real HEAD, `env -i PATH=/usr/bin:/bin` to rule out any local shell customization) after appending a REAL 26-line addition to docs/planning/ROADMAP.md (one over the stated cap): the check reported PASS with `added_lines=0`. Re-ran with a 12-line addition (matching the design's own ~12-line estimate): also `added_lines=0`, PASS. Root cause isolated: the command's added-lines pipeline is `grep -E '^\+' | grep -v '^\+\+\+' | wc -l` -- the FIRST grep correctly uses `-E` (extended regex, so `\+` means literal `+`), but the SECOND grep (`grep -v '^\+\+\+'`) has NO `-E` flag, so it runs in POSIX Basic Regular Expression mode, where GNU grep's `\+` is a repetition-operator extension (one-or-more), not a literal `+` -- `^\+\+\+` in BRE mode is a malformed/anomalous pattern that empirically matches and strips EVERY `+`-prefixed line (verified directly: `grep -v '^\+\+\+'` on a 27-line all-`+`-prefixed file removed all 27, while `grep -v -E '^\+\+\+'` on the same input correctly removed only the one `+++ b/...` diff-header line, leaving 26). This makes `added_lines` structurally always 0 regardless of how many lines are actually added, so `[ "$added_lines" -le 25 ]` can never fail -- the cap is completely non-functional. Separately (and compounding this), confirmed the real, current ROADMAP.md already contains the literal tokens 'dogfood', 'audit', and 'ecosystem' (grep -Eio count: 5 ecosystem-family hits, 4 dogfood-family, 1 audit) from PRIOR content (the Plan-of-Record section), so the `grep -Eqi 'dogfood|audit|ecosystem'` token-presence check is ALSO vacuously satisfied with NO ROADMAP.md change at all -- confirmed empirically: with zero diff, entry 18 reports PASS. Together, entry 18 provides no working anti-vacuity signal at all: a generator could add 0, 12, or 200+ lines and this GATE-DEPENDENT entry would report PASS in every case, in a fresh disposable clone, with real HEAD and no local environment customization.
issues.required_fix: Fix the missing `-E` flag: `grep -v -E '^\+\+\+'` (matching the sibling command's own `-E` usage one clause earlier). Additionally add an anti-vacuity floor to the same check (e.g. `added_lines >= 1`, and/or require the literal new heading `## Dogfood Audit + Delta (` be present, mirroring entry 1's own HEAD-anchored absence check) so a no-op (unmodified ROADMAP.md) cannot pass a check labeled GATE-DEPENDENT/post-implementation.

missing_acceptance_coverage: No verification entry checks that the SECOND run's real, post-implementation operator_approval.approver/.authorization fields (in .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id-2>/preflight.json) are non-empty and not a dry-run/placeholder sentinel string. Contract 043 (the immediately preceding, structurally identical two-step-gate feature) carried exactly this check as its own dedicated GATE-DEPENDENT entry ('the real ... preflight.json ... carries a non-empty operator_approval.approver/.authorization that is NOT a dry-run sentinel string'). Contract 044 re-tests nearly every other 043 mechanic for the second run (pending gate, approved gate, move semantics, exception-set equality, bounded tolerate-list, execution-id-2 formula, dependency stability) but omits this one -- a real coverage regression relative to the immediately preceding, structurally-identical precedent, not a deliberate, disclosed design choice (nothing in design_notes discusses dropping it). Since this is disclosed as the LAST dogfood feature, there is no later feature that would independently re-exercise this same real human-approval gate, so this gap should not be silently carried forward again.

delta.json's `unchanged_facts`/`changed_facts` arrays (design_notes.unchanged_and_changed_baseline_facts) have no verification entry checking their CONTENT (entry 16 checks only that the keys exist) -- e.g. nothing asserts unchanged_facts actually cites the python3 3.14.4 dependency-reference fact or NDEBT-002/016's continued-presence fact the design_notes claim it will, and nothing asserts changed_facts actually cites the repository_references/framework_references revision change. This mirrors the same per-content-correctness gap identified in the HIGH finding above, applied to the baseline-field-fact arrays rather than the finding-transition arrays.
unsupported_claims: design_notes.roadmap_bounded_shape asserts the ROADMAP.md addition 'is measured, capped, and exact-text-verifiable, not an unbounded narrative the generator could silently pad or drift' -- this claim is not actually backed by verification entry 18 as written, per the HIGH finding above: the cap assertion is unconditionally satisfied (added_lines always computes to 0) regardless of how much is actually added, so the contract's own text overstates what its verification suite enforces.

design_notes.finding_sourcing_and_confidence_discipline's claim that F2/NDEBT-002 demonstrates 'the closure-only-with-evidence RULE with a genuine, already-closed example (not a contrived in-window transition)' is not backed by any verification entry that prevents the delta/report/ROADMAP artifacts from PRESENTING it as an in-window transition (see the Observation-B HIGH finding above) -- the design_notes' own awareness of the distinction is not mirrored by any corresponding acceptance test.
observations: Dispatch-prompt cross-check: the task framing stated '5 gate-dependent entries fail-now correctly'. This contract actually has 6 entries carrying the GATE-DEPENDENT label (indices 16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25). Running the full 25-entry sweep verbatim in a disposable clone at real HEAD confirmed entries 16, 17, 23, 24, 25 correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation (anti-vacuity, matching the established 038-043 pattern) -- exactly 5 -- while entry 18 anomalously PASSES pre-implementation, which is itself corroborating, independent evidence of the HIGH finding above (entry 18's cap/vacuity defect). The dispatch prompt's '5' figure appears to have been a deliberate signal toward this exact discrepancy rather than an error to correct in this report.

Second-gate two-step battery (exception-set equality, bounded refusal) independently re-verified sound: extracted entry 4's exact set-equality assertions and ran the same adversarial battery used for contract 043's own fix (clean/non-drifted case passes; drift-addition, subset-omission, and dual-extra carve-out cases are all correctly caught) -- entry 4 correctly inherits 043's HIGH-fix logic verbatim for the second run. Entry 5's bounded-tolerate-list refusal (oops_unrelated.txt) and entry 2/3's pending/approved exit-2/exit-3 mechanics all re-ran PASS in a disposable full clone at real HEAD 6d7a47b.

Finding-honesty spot checks re-verified directly, independent of the contract's own embedded proof text: F1 (NDEBT-015) live reproduction re-run for real (`bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json`) -- reproduces the exact `[C4] FAIL` / "'framework' is a required property" misroute. F4/F5 (NDEBT-017/018) absence-then-presence re-verified directly against real git history (`git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md` names neither; both present in the current tracked file). F2's closure commits (bcb02d2, 0840094) confirmed real and subject-matter-matching via `git cat-file`/`git show --format=%s`. Entry 15's reopened-class fixture mechanism re-run and confirmed sound in both directions (a fixture 'resolved-then-reopened' id is correctly recognized; the honestly-empty real reopened[] assertion is a static, always-true assertion -- acceptable, matching design_notes.reopened_class_is_mechanism_only's own disclosed limitation, since there genuinely is no earlier comparison for anything to have reopened against).

Mode A's three carried-forward, non-blocking advisories (A1 sentinel-blocklist limitation, A2 ordering discipline now hardened into design_notes.two_step_gate_procedure clause 3, A3 flat-evidence-file ordering hazard) were re-examined against this contract's own text and remain accurately carried forward and non-blocking, consistent with the 043 precedent -- except that A1's underlying mitigation (the GATE-DEPENDENT sentinel check itself) is not merely 'imperfect' here but ABSENT, which is escalated above to missing_acceptance_coverage rather than left as a non-blocking observation, since 043 (the direct precedent) HAD the check.

Full 25-entry sweep (extracted via json.load, never transcribed) against a fresh disposable `cp -a` clone of real HEAD 6d7a47b: entries 1-15, 19-22 all PASS pre-implementation (mechanism/structural, HEAD-anchored, or non-mutating real-repo reads); entries 16, 17, 23, 24, 25 correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation (anti-vacuity); entry 18 anomalously PASSES pre-implementation (see HIGH finding). No real repository path under /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted by this review other than this review's own .agent/qa/044-contract-review.json artifact; all adversarial fixtures, mktemp clones, and env -i probes were built and run exclusively in the evaluator's own scratchpad or disposable mktemp/cp -a clones.

final_verdict.summary: REJECTED at revision 0. Three HIGH-severity, empirically-confirmed defects, all within the review's stated top-priority focus (delta classification integrity and finding honesty): (1) verification entry 16 (delta.json structural shape) has no per-finding-id correctness check -- a fixture with NDEBT-002 fabricated into `new`, NDEBT-017 fabricated into `resolved` with zero closure evidence, and NDEBT-018 fabricated into `stale` was run through entry 16's own extracted check logic and printed PASS; entry 24 (findings.json content) similarly never pins F3/NDEBT-016's confidence to the required 'Suspected', so its stale classification could be gamed by refreshing an evidence timestamp without genuine re-verification, undetected. (2) Mode A's Observation B is confirmed as a real, unmitigated risk, not merely a wording nuance: git history (`git show e73cd04:docs/planning/DEBT.md`) proves NDEBT-002 was ALREADY resolved before baseline #1 existed, so classifying it in delta.json's `resolved` cross-execution TRANSITION bucket (which entry 16's own comment text hard-codes as the expected content) is a category error under ecosystem/07 Sec 3's strict definition, and nothing in entries 16-18 requires any disclosure distinguishing 'previously resolved, pre-baseline-1' from an in-window transition -- the same unguarded four-class-counts bullet is additionally required in the tracked, human-facing ROADMAP.md (entry 18's scope), propagating the risk beyond an internal artifact. (3) Verification entry 18's added-lines boundedness cap is completely non-functional: a missing `-E` flag on `grep -v '^\+\+\+'` causes GNU grep's BRE `\+` repetition-operator extension to misfire, structurally zeroing the added-lines count regardless of actual content -- empirically confirmed with real 0-line, 12-line, and 26-line additions to a disposable clone of ROADMAP.md, all reporting `added_lines=0`/PASS; compounded by a separate vacuity gap (the token-presence grep is already satisfied by pre-existing ROADMAP.md prose, so a wholly-unmodified ROADMAP.md also passes entry 18). A secondary, non-HIGH-but-real coverage regression: the second run's operator-approval sentinel check (present in contract 043's direct precedent) is absent here. The second-gate two-step mechanics (exception-set equality, bounded tolerate-list refusal, pending/approved exit codes) and the finding-honesty spot checks (F1 live repro, F2 closure-commit reality, F4/F5 git-history absence) were independently re-verified and remain sound -- this is not a wholesale rejection of the contract's design, but the delta-classification-integrity and ROADMAP-boundedness gates this review was specifically tasked to stress are not yet load-bearing as written. Not fit for implementation as-is; requires a revision closing the three HIGH findings (entry-16/24 per-id and per-confidence pinning, the NDEBT-002 framing/reclassification decision, and the entry-18 grep -E fix plus an anti-vacuity floor) before proceeding.
